title: Matcha-Glazed and Lacquer-Wiped Ethiopian Beef Kitfo-Style Tibs
description: Resinous, pillowy, ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly Ethiopian beef dish, kitfo-form, hot-stone-plate, matcha-glazed and lacquer-wiped, meal prep ready.

ingredients.name: beef (kitfo-form, thinly sliced)
ingredients.notes: high-protein, tamasic-friendly, choose red meat for authentic flavor

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: for matcha glaze, adjust to desired consistency

ingredients.name: turmeric
ingredients.notes: tamasic-friendly spice, for color and flavor

ingredients.name: ghee or oil
ingredients.notes: for hot-stone-plate prep, choose ghee for authentic flavor

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.notes: for lacquer glaze, choose high-quality for best flavor

instructions: Prepare beef by slicing into kitfo-form, thinly sliced.
Season beef with salt, black pepper, and turmeric.
In a pan, heat ghee or oil over medium heat, then sauté onion and garlic until lightly browned.
Add beef to the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and cooked to desired level of doneness.
Transfer beef to a plate, leaving excess oil in the pan.
In a small bowl, whisk together matcha powder, sugar, and water to create a smooth matcha glaze.
Pour matcha glaze over beef and spread evenly.
In a separate pan, melt butter over medium heat, then brush with a lacquer glaze consistency.
Wipe lacquer glaze over beef, creating a resinous sheen.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped onion and garlic if desired.
Serve with injera or your preferred bread for a traditional Ethiopian experience.
